_doc: CANONICAL source of truth for the per-agent plugin manifests. Hand-edit THIS file; the .claude-plugin/ and .codex-plugin/ manifests are GENERATED from it by `node build.mjs` (adapters/*.mjs). `node build.mjs --check` fails if a generated manifest drifts from this source. See docs/strategy/agent-harness-overhaul-2026-07-09/ws-a-design.md.

claude.description: Linlin's curated Claude Code configuration: workflow rules, skills, hooks, plugin recommendations, tooling preferences, and project templates. Install once, then run /init-agent-config in any new project to scaffold it with the relevant subset.
notes: Claude Code uses .claude-plugin/plugin.json. Codex uses .codex-plugin/plugin.json and root hooks.json; keeping them separate preserves Claude Code behavior while making the adapter discoverable by Codex.
When either plugin spec changes, update the corresponding manifest without removing fields used by the other agent's discovery path.

codex.description: Codex adapter for Linlin's agent-harness workflows, reusable skills, project setup guidance, hooks, recommendations, tooling preferences, templates, and safe Codex update repair.

interface.longDescription: Use agent-harness from Codex without changing the original Claude Code setup. The plugin exposes Codex-compatible setup, verification, visual review, research critique, privacy redaction, system cleanup, long-running task, safe Codex update, and cross-agent adaptation workflows while keeping Claude-specific files isolated.
defaultPrompt: Apply agent-harness to this project for Codex
Activate agent-harness as my default local Codex plugin
Repair a Codex CLI update that failed because latest release assets were unavailable

opencode._doc: opencode has NO static plugin manifest (plugins are JS). We emit a real opencode.json (JSONC-compatible). rules -> instructions[] (always-on context). SKILLS are NOT loaded via instructions: opencode reads skills natively from its skill dir ~/.config/opencode/skills/ (verified against opencode 1.3.17 — there is NO project-level .claude/skills or .opencode/skill auto-discovery), so they are DEPLOYED there by bin/deploy-skills.mjs (on-demand, not always-on). model/small_model are editable defaults (task 10). Source: opencode.ai/docs + real-binary verification 2026-07-10.
opencode._model_note: model / small_model are NOT set here — they are generated from adapters/models.config.json (agents.opencode.high / .small) so task 10 has ONE source of truth for model tiers.
opencode._instructions_note: SKILLS are handled by bin/deploy-skills.mjs (native ~/.config/opencode/skills/), NOT here. instructions[] is for always-on RULE.md context only. The earlier '.claude/skills/*/SKILL.md' entry was WRONG — in-repo that glob matches only the 3 new-* templates, and loading skills as always-on instructions defeats on-demand token savings; removed.
opencode._plugin_note: opencode auto-discovers local project plugins from .opencode/plugin/ (SINGULAR — verified against the opencode 1.3.17 binary and its own tip 'Add .ts/.js files to .opencode/plugin/ for event hooks'; the plural .opencode/plugins/ is NOT scanned). So ship the plugin file there and it needs no `plugin[]` entry. `plugin[]` is only for npm/git-published external modules (e.g. the user's live config loads superpowers via git); we have none, so it stays empty. (Earlier this note wrongly said plural — that mistake left review-gate.js in an unscanned dir so it never loaded.)
